---
title: "Haploid analysis DR. Alice Godden"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

First- PCA analysis for multiple groups and multiple male samples for the sperm pools analysis

```{r}
# load tidyverse package
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# read in the eigenvec file
pca <- read.table("spermpoolhighdp_pca.eigenvec", header=FALSE)

# plot the first two principal components with axes labeled in percentages
ggplot(pca, aes(x=V3, y=V4)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format())

# read in eigenvalues
eigenval <- scan("spermpoolhighdp_pca.eigenval")


# convert eigenvalues to percentage variance explained
pve <- data.frame(PC = 1:10, pve = eigenval/sum(eigenval)*100)

# sort out the pca data
pca <- pca[,-1]
names(pca)[1] <- "ind"
names(pca)[2:ncol(pca)] <- paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-1))
pca <- as.tibble(data.frame(pca))


# Read in the metadata file
metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv")

# Merge the metadata with the PCA file by sample ID
merged_data <- inner_join(metadata, pca, by=c("Sample"="ind"))

merged_data2 <-merged_data[,-1]


# Filter the data to include only specific groups
filtered_data <- subset(merged_data2, Male %in% c("31", "32", "34"))

# Convert PC1 and PC2 to numeric
filtered_data$PC1 <- as.numeric(filtered_data$PC1)
filtered_data$PC2 <- as.numeric(filtered_data$PC2)

# Plot with color-coded points and shape-coded by Group
ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Male, shape = Group)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.05, height = 0.05) +  # Add jitter to points to avoid overlap
  labs(title = "PCA Plot", x = "PC1 48.8%", y = "PC2 25.6%") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) 



# Define colors for Male values
male_colors <- c("31" = "blue", "32" = "red", "34" = "green")

# Filter the data to include only specific groups
filtered_data <- subset(merged_data2, Male %in% c("31", "32", "34"))

# Convert PC1 and PC2 to numeric
filtered_data$PC1 <- as.numeric(filtered_data$PC1)
filtered_data$PC2 <- as.numeric(filtered_data$PC2)

# Plot with color-coded points and shape-coded by Group
ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Male, shape = Group)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0.1) +  # Add jitter to points to avoid overlap
  labs(title = "PCA Plot", x = "PC1 48.8%", y = "PC2 25.6%") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_color_viridis(discrete = FALSE)

# 

# Convert Male to a factor variable
filtered_data$Male <- factor(filtered_data$Male)

# Define custom colors for each Male group
male_colors <- c("31" = "royalblue2", "32" = "indianred", "34" = "orange")

# Plot with custom colors for Male groups
ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Male, shape = Group)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.2, alpha = 0.6) +  # Add jitter to points to avoid overlap
  labs(title = "PCA Plot Sperm Phenotypic data", x = "PC1 48.8%", y = "PC2 25.6%") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_color_manual(values = male_colors) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_bw() +  # Set the background to white
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black"),
        legend.text = element_text(size=16, face = "bold", color = "black"),
        
        #panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  # Remove grid lines
        #panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(),  # Add axis lines
        axis.ticks = element_line())  # Add tick marks


```

PCA plotting for the Heat stress project
```{r}
# load tidyverse package
library(tidyverse)
library(ggrepel)
library(viridis)
###########3USEUSUESUEUSUESUEUSUEUSUE
# read in the eigenvec file
pca <- read.table("spermpoolhighdp_pca.eigenvec", header=FALSE)

# plot the first two principal components with axes labeled in percentages
ggplot(pca, aes(x=V3, y=V4)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format())

# read in eigenvalues
eigenval <- scan("spermpoolhighdp_pca.eigenval")


# convert eigenvalues to percentage variance explained
pve <- data.frame(PC = 1:10, pve = eigenval/sum(eigenval)*100)
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pve, "percentVar"))



library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)



# sort out the pca data
pca <- pca[,-1]
names(pca)[1] <- "ind"
names(pca)[2:ncol(pca)] <- paste0("PC", 1:(ncol(pca)-1))
pca <- as.tibble(data.frame(pca))


# Read in the metadata file
metadata <- read.csv("metadata.csv")

# Merge the metadata with the PCA file by sample ID
merged_data <- inner_join(metadata, pca, by=c("Sample"="ind"))

merged_data2 <-merged_data[,-1]




#####JUST one group etc


# Filter the data to include only specific groups
filtered_data <- subset(merged_data2, Male %in% c("31", "32", "34"))

ggplot(filtered_data, aes(x=PC1, y=PC2, color=Male, shape=Group)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title="PCA Plot", x="PC1 48.8%", y="PC2 25.6%") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format())



# Use viridis color palette
ggplot(filtered_data, aes(PC1, PC2, color=Group)) +
  geom_point(size=4, alpha=0.5) +

  scale_color_viridis(discrete = TRUE) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=seq(0,15,18)) +
  xlab(paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance")) + 
  #stat_ellipse() +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_bw() +  # Set the background to white
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size=16, face = "bold"),
        
        #panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  # Remove grid lines
        #panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.line = element_line(),  # Add axis lines
        axis.ticks = element_line())  # Add tick marks

```

Making Manhattan plots for FST analyses. If you did windowed FST file will be smaller, if non-windowed FST analysis plotting takes longer, use Cairo etc to plot these.
```{r}
#Load libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(grDevices)
library(qqman)
library(base)
library(knitr)
library(rmarkdown)
library(Cairo)
library(png)


#set the working directory
setwd("~/F0F3_Haploid/Upload/Split_ARRAY2/PCA/fst_snpsonly")

fst3 <- read.table("fst_outputmale_MT_V_MC.50kbwindowed.weir.fst", sep='\t', header =TRUE)

length_ <- dim(fst3)[1]
length_
fst3$SNP <- paste('SNP',1:length_)


# Set the physical size of the image in inches
CairoPNG('manhattan_plot_male.png', width = 800/100, height = 600/100, units = 'in', res = 300)

# Create the plot with the same dimensions and aspect ratio as the image
c <- manhattan(fst3, chr = 'CHROM', bp = 'BIN_START', 
               p = "WEIGHTED_FST", snp = 'SNP',
               col = c("lightblue", "navy"), suggestiveline = F, 
               genomewideline = 0.05, 
              logp = FALSE, 
               ylim = c(0.0, 0.8), ylab = 'WEIGHTED FST',
               xlab = 'CHROMOSOME', main = 'FST Testes windowed 50kb') +
  theme(
        axis.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
#plot
c



# Set the physical size of the image in inches
CairoPNG('manhattan_plot_F1_male_windowed_100kb_lat_sat.png', width = 800/100, height = 600/100, units = 'in', res = 300)

# Create the plot with the same dimensions and aspect ratio as the image
c <- manhattan(fst3, chr = 'CHROM', bp = 'BIN_START', 
               p = "MEAN_FST", snp = 'SNP', 
               col = c("black", "red"), suggestiveline = F, 
               genomewideline = 0.1, logp = FALSE, 
               ylim = c(0.0, 0.1), ylab = 'WEIR AND COCKERHAM_FST',
               xlab = 'BIN_START', main = 'FST F1_LAT_V_SAT_male_windowed_100kb')

manhattan(fst3, chr = "CHROM", col = c("blue", "red"), cex = 0.6)
# Copy the plot to the PNG device
dev.copy(png, 'manhattan_plot_F1_Female_windowed_100kb_lat_sat2.png')

# Close the PNG device
dev.off()
```

FST, TajimaD and plots with genes and transposons tracks. The tracks are auto annotated from bed and gtf files that have been set up as follows-
```{r}
#Navigate to UCSC table browser
#https://genome.ucsc.edu/cgi-bin/hgTables?hgsid=1565197003_reKB5JqDAhJVKIOckmt1Ss7mZeaT&clade=vertebrate&org=Zebrafish&db=danRer11&hgta_group=allTracks&hgta_track=rmsk&hgta_table=0&hgta_regionType=genome&position=chr6%3A43%2C426%2C669-43%2C433%2C274&hgta_outputType=gff&hgta_outFileName=test
#Choose zebrafish genome, track Repeatmasker for TEs, and Genes and Gene predictions, track Ensembl genes for genes. Save and download the file as a .gft file.
#On command line grep the chromosome you want
#removed duplicate rows/redundant information
```

Centromere locations and chromosome lengths
```{r}
#Centromeres and chromosome lengths
# 1 33133433 59578282
# 2 19743539 59640629
# 3 49120158 62628489
# 4 25975534 78093715
# 5 50391699 72500376
# 6 34866988 60270059
# 7 60898697 74282399
# 8 30099230 54304671
# 9 13283057 56459846
# 10 9894335 45420867
# 11 9507769 45484837
# 12 27779269 49182954
# 13 19555524 52186027
# 14 16933009 52660232
# 15 13943685 48040578
# 16 19463555 55266484
# 17 48225166 53461100
# 18 24528805 51023478
# 19 19777257 48449771
# 20 11319545 55201332
# 21 28908825 45934066
# 22 21520944 39133080
# 23 13631408 46223584
# 24 15196971 42172926
# 25 20793603 37502051
```

Making the plots for TajD and FST with gene and TE tracks
```{r}
library(qvalue)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)
library(Cairo)
library(png)

# 1. Open pdf file
#pdf("TESTING2.pdf", width = "350", height = "350")
# 2. Create the plot

# Read gene data from CSV files
gene_data_black <- read.csv("TE_data_chr18.filtered.csv", header = TRUE)
gene_data_red <- read.csv("18genes.filtered.csv", header = TRUE)

# Read in the FST values
fst_data <- read.table("fst_outputfemale_FT_V_FC.windowed.weir.fst", header = TRUE)

# Rename colname 3 to FST for weir fst
colnames(fst_data)[5] <- "FST"

# Choose the chromosome you want to plot
chromosome <- "18"

# Subset the data for the chosen chromosome
chromosome_data <- subset(fst_data, CHROM == chromosome)

# Read data from a text file
TajimaD <- read.table("Taj_FT_FC.Tajima.D", header = TRUE)

# Rename colname 4 to TajD
colnames(TajimaD)[4] <- "TajD"
tajima_data <- TajimaD

# Remove NA's from Tajima D data
complete_rows <- complete.cases(tajima_data)
subset_dataTD <- tajima_data[complete_rows, ]

# Subset the data for the chosen chromosome
chromosomeT <- "18"
chromosome_dataTD <- subset(subset_dataTD, CHROM == chromosomeT)

combined_data <- data.frame(BIN_START = chromosome_data$BIN_START,
                            FST = chromosome_data$FST,
                            Tajima_D = chromosome_dataTD$TajD)

# Create the plot
c <- ggplot(data = combined_data, aes(x = BIN_START)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 24528805, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey16") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.00, linetype = "solid", color = "black") +
  geom_line(aes(y = Tajima_D * 0.1), color = "skyblue", linetype = "solid") +
  geom_point(aes(y = FST), color = "navy") +
  
  
  geom_segment(data = gene_data_black, aes(x = start, xend = end, y = -0.35, yend = -0.37), color = "gray30", alpha=0.7) +
  geom_segment(data = gene_data_red, aes(x = start, xend = end, y = -0.3, yend = -0.35), color = "orangered", alpha=0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(data = gene_data_black, aes(x = (start + end) / 2, y = -0.04, label = FALSE), vjust = 1, color = "black", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text_repel(data = gene_data_red, aes(x = (start + end) / 2, y = -0.05, label = FALSE), vjust = 1, color = "red", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  
  labs(title = expression(bold("Chr 18 WEIR FST Ovaries Temperature vs Control 50kb wind.", size = 20))) + # Bold text for title +
  xlab(expression(bold("Window Position"))) +
  ylim(-0.2, 0.8) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "white"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
        axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
        axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", face = 'bold', size = 15),
        plot.margin = margin(0.4, 0.4, 0.4, 0.4, "cm"),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white")) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "on") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "FST",
                     sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / 0.1, name = "Tajima D",
                                         breaks = seq(-3, 5, by = 0.5),
                                         labels = seq(-3, 5, by = 0.5))) +
  theme(axis.title.y.left = element_text(color = "navy", face = 'bold', size = 15),
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "skyblue", face = 'bold', size = 15))

plot(c)
# Save the plot as a PDF file
ggsave("TESTING10.pdf", plot = c, width = 10, height = 8)

# Close the PDF device
#dev.off()

```
